_readme: Central assumptions config. Every model parameter lives here with its citation. The methodology page is generated from this file. Slider defaults and ranges defined here; changing a default never requires touching engine code.

wage.citation: Placeholder anchor between the Levy Institute's $15 (2018 dollars, WP 902) and current living-wage estimates (~$19-21 single adult in median states). PENDING: replace with per-state living wage anchor upon Living Wage Institute license, or BEA Regional Price Parity scaling.
wage.annual_hours_citation: Full-time convention: 40 hrs/wk x 52 wks, matching MIT Living Wage Calculator and Levy methodology.

benefits_load.citation: Levy WP 902 assumes ~20% of wages for benefits incl. health insurance. BLS ECEC benchmarks private-sector benefits at 29-32% of total compensation (higher because it includes legally required payroll taxes; our admin category partially overlaps).

participation_rate.citation: Share of officially unemployed who take a program job. No historical precedent for exact uptake; Brookings/Hamilton Project 2018 analysis models participation varying with wage level and geography. 60% default is a conservative midpoint; slider exists precisely because this is uncertain.

non_labor_cost_markup.citation: Levy WP 902: materials, equipment, and other non-labor program costs modeled at 25% of the wage bill.

efficiency_multiplier.citation: Public-vs-private delivery cost adjustment applied to non-labor costs. 1.0 = private benchmarks. Range reflects contractor margins of only 3-7% on the low end and documented US public-sector cost premiums (transit-costs literature) on the high end. Deliberately a slider, not an assumption: the tool's answer to 'government is inefficient' is to let skeptics set it to 130% and see the program still function.

admin_overhead.citation: Program administration at 2-5% of program cost, consistent with existing large federal programs (e.g., Social Security admin <1%, Medicaid ~5%, UI ~3%).

fiscal_offset_rate.citation: Levy Institute estimates net program cost 20-25% below gross via reduced UI/Medicaid/EITC spending and increased tax revenue; Levy authors note these are conservative (exclude incarceration, other savings). Default 22% = midpoint.

labor_share_crosscheck.citation: PRELIMINARY midpoints of literature ranges (see data/processed/labor_share_coefficients.json). Used only for the ASCE-dollars-to-jobs cross-check, not the primary Levy-method engine. Replace with exact BEA GDP-by-Industry extraction before launch.

childcare_staffing_ratio.citation: PRELIMINARY blended ratio across age groups (licensing ratios run ~4:1 infants to ~10:1 preschool). Converts the 4.2M-child care gap into required workers. Replace with per-state licensing-ratio-weighted figure.

state_allocation_proxy.citation: v1 proxy: national ASCE category gaps allocated to states by share of national labor force. Documented limitation; v2 upgrades to per-category proxies (FHWA lane-miles for roads, NBI bridge counts for bridges, etc.).
